matches.snippet: seized his pullets and primed their plumages, the fionnling and dubhlet, the dun and the fire
matches.reasoning: fionnling is diminutive/variant of Fionn; appears with dubhlet (dub = dark, dialectal for 'black'), reinforcing the binary structure of Fianna warrior pairs

matches.snippet: Fingool MacKishgmard Obesume Burgearse Benefice, He was bowen hem and scrapin him
matches.reasoning: Fingool is transparent garbling of Fingal/Finn + MacCool; the patronymic structure (Mac+) invokes Fionn mac Cumhaill directly

matches.snippet: He knows his Finsbury Follies backwoods so you batter see to your regent refutation
matches.reasoning: Finsbury (London street/area) puns on Finn + bury; follies compound the pun (follies = jester's pranks, Finn's trickster aspect)

matches.snippet: Two stick hoist. Lucky! Finnish Make Goal! First you were Nomad, next you were Namar
matches.reasoning: Finnish invokes Finn directly; Make Goal likely puns on MacCool/Mac + goal; part of series of identity transformations (Nomad/Namar/Numah/Nomon)

matches.matched_text: The Fenn, the Fenn, the finn of all Fenns
matches.snippet: Clanruckard for ever! The Fenn, the Fenn, the finn of all Fenns! Deaf to the winds
matches.reasoning: Explicit invocation of Fenn/Fenns (the Fianna band); 'finn of all Fenns' is direct reference to Fionn as chief of the Fianna; triple repetition emphasizes mythic status

matches.snippet: Drawg us out Ivy Eve in the Hall of Alum! The finnecies of poetry wed music. Feeling the jitters?
matches.reasoning: finnecies puns on Finn + finesse/fancies; in context of poetry/music/wedding, invokes Finn's role as poet-warrior and courtly figure
